https://amp.cnn.com/cnn/cnn-underscored/.description: Even in an age of digital financial statements and paperless billing, a paper shredder has a place in your home. Banking records, health information and other private documents are often still sent to your home’s mailbox. Shredding this sensitive information into tiny, difficult-to-read scraps before tossing it out can help protect your identity (and your money) from would-be thieves.
